coredata.dc:title: β-Cyclodextrin functionalized N,Zn codoped carbon dots for specific fluorescence detection of fluoroquinolones in milk samples
coredata.dc:creator: Zhu, Yueran
Lu, Yunguang
Shi, Longyan
Yang, Yaling
coredata.dc:description: A green, reliable and simple assay for Fluoroquinolone derivatives (FQs) is developed based on Mono-(6-Amino-6-Deoxy)-β-Cyclodextrin (β-CD) were modified on the surface of N,Zn co-doped carbon dots (N,Zn-CDs). FQs not only enhance β-CD-N,Zn-CDs fluorescence intensity, but also red-shift. Since FQs has the same effect on the fluorescence of β-CD-N,Zn-CDs, so ofloxacin (OFL) as a representative for analysis. Under optimized detection conditions, the linear range is 0.075–3.75 μM and the LOD is 0.05 μM (S/N = =3). Finally, the proposed method was successfully applied in the determination of OFL in milk and domestic water with satisfactory recoveries.
coredata.dcterms:subject: β-CD-N,Zn-CDs
Fluoroquinolones
Fluorescence detection

xocs:item-toc-entry.xocs:item-toc-section-title: OFL detection in milk and domestic water sample
TEM, XRD and XPS analysis of N,Zn-CDs
Detection of antibiotics in actual samples

ce:caption.ce:simple-para: TME image and size distribution of N,Zn-CDs.
(a) Fluorescence excitation and emission spectra of β-CD-N,Zn-CDs nanoprobes. (b) β-CD-N,Zn-CDs fluorescence spectra λex = =300–420 nm.

(a) Compared to CD and β-CD-N, Zn-CD fluorescence intensity is significantly enhanced. (b) β-CD-N,Zn-CDs and 2 μM OFL and OFL The uv-vis absorption. β-CD-N,Zn-CDs adding OFL generation of chromophores.
Illustration of detection strategy of FQs detection using β-CD-N,Zn-CDs nanoprobe via host–guest interaction.
Analytical results of the OFL detection real sample (n = 3).
The comparison of different analytical methods for the determination of OFL.

head.ce:title: β-Cyclodextrin functionalized N,Zn codoped carbon dots for specific fluorescence detection of fluoroquinolones in milk samples

ce:affiliation.ce:label: a
ce:affiliation.ce:textfn: Faculty of Life Science and Technology, Kunming University of Science and Technology, Yunnan Province 650500, China

sa:affiliation.sa:organization: Faculty of Life Science and Technology
Kunming University of Science and Technology

ce:affiliation.ce:source-text: aFaculty of Life Science and Technology, Kunming University of Science and Technology, Yunnan Province 650500, China

ce:affiliation.ce:label: b
ce:affiliation.ce:textfn: Yunnan Jianniu Bio Technology Co., Ltd., Kunming, 650033, China

sa:affiliation.sa:organization: Yunnan Jianniu Bio Technology Co., Ltd.

ce:affiliation.ce:source-text: bYunnan Jianniu Bio Technology Co., Ltd., Kunming, 650033, China

ce:abstract-sec.ce:simple-para: A green, reliable and simple assay for Fluoroquinolone derivatives (FQs) is developed based on Mono-(6-Amino-6-Deoxy)-β-Cyclodextrin (β-CD) were modified on the surface of N,Zn co-doped carbon dots (N,Zn-CDs). FQs not only enhance β-CD-N,Zn-CDs fluorescence intensity, but also red-shift. Since FQs has the same effect on the fluorescence of β-CD-N,Zn-CDs, so ofloxacin (OFL) as a representative for analysis. Under optimized detection conditions, the linear range is 0.075–3.75 μM and the LOD is 0.05 μM (S/N = =3). Finally, the proposed method was successfully applied in the determination of OFL in milk and domestic water with satisfactory recoveries.

ce:section.ce:para: Fluoroquinolones (FQs) are a group of broad-spectrum anti-bacterial agents used by people to protect their lives and health in order to kill bacterial infections [1]. FQs are also widely used in animal husbandry and agriculture [2–4]. Unfortunately, excessive intake of antibiotics in the human body can cause serious adverse reactions such as allergies, visual impairment and brain damage [5–7]. Many residual antibiotic detection methods had been developed for this purpose. Among them, the chromatographic method using solid-phase extraction (SPE) as a pre-concentration step is widely used [8]. However, the drawbacks of being laborious and time-consuming limited their application. As an alternative, high-throughput immunoassay is a good choice for analysis of FQs from water samples, Based on their excellent performance, the assays could screen containments from large amounts of samples in a short period of time [9,10]. But the methods cannot be done with an automated instrument, and cannot carry out on-site analysis for the organic pollutants because of relatively sophisticated procedures.

β-CD is a cyclic polysaccharide having a hydrophilic outer surface and a hydrophobic inner cavity, which are composed of seven glucose units linked by α-1,4 bonds [11–13]. Carbon dots are a new member of the carbon nanomaterial family with a size of less than 10 nm [14], good biocompatibility, low toxicity and fluorescence [15,16]. Therefore, CDs are widely used in chemical/biosensors [17–21], such as fluorescence detection technology [22–24].

In this work, N,Zn-CDs were modified with amino-containing β-CD to form an amide bond, and β-CD-N,Zn-CDs nanoprobes were prepared. β-CD not only promotes electron transfer, but also increases the specific surface area of N,Zn-CDs, therefore increasing the selectivity of N,Zn-CDs. As showed in Scheme 1, FQs enhanced the fluorescence of the nanoprobe and the fluorescence intensity red-shifted with an increase in the FQs concentration. So fluorescence intensity is correlated with FQs concentration. It can be used for quantitative detection. The fluorescence detection method is green and environmental protection, Generation of a large volume of analytical waste be avoided, and is easy to operate, in line with the 12 principles of green analytical chemistry (GAC) [25]. This method without expensive or sophisticated instrumentation, extensive sample preparation, and more skilled person required. The results show that the β-CD-N,Zn-CDs is suitable for the detection of OFL in actual samples.

All chemicals obtained from commercial suppliers were utilized without further purification. ZnSO4, HCl, FeCl3, CuCl2, MgCl2, CaCl2, KCl2, were purchased from Sinopharm Chemical Reagent Co., Ltd. L-cysteine (L-Cys), ofloxacin (OFL), norfloxacin (NOR), Ciprofloxacin(CIP), gentamicin (CN), roxithromycin (ROX), moxilin (AML), penicillin G (GP), ethylenediamine ceftiofur (EFT), N-hydroxysuccinimide (NHS), 1-ethyl-3-(3-dimethylaminopropyl) carbodiimide hydrochloride (EDC⋅HCl) and quinine sulfate were provided from Aladdin Chemistry Co. Ltd. Mono-(6-Amino-6-Deoxy)-β-Cyclodextrin (98.0%) was obtained from Zhiyuan Bio-Technology Co. Ltd (Shandong, China). Milk purchased from the Europe-Asia company, (Yunnan China, batch number, 20190618).

Transmission electron microscopic (TEM) images were investigated for all tested samples using a JEM-2100 microscope to investigate the microstructures and size distribution. Chemical bonds and components of were investigated using an IR Prestige-21 (FT-IR) in the frequency range of 4000–500 cm−1 and an ESCALAB 250 Xi X-ray photoelectron spectrometer (XPS). Fluorescence spectra recorded on F-2500 (Hitachi, Japan) fluorescence spectrophotometer, respectively. X-ray diffraction (XRD) using a Bruker AXS D8-discover. Analytik Jena AG TOP was used for microwave digestion.

ZnSO4 (0.5 g), concentrated H2SO4 (0.9 mL), and ethylene diamine (2.65 mL) dissolved in water (25 mL) in ultrasound for 5 min to make it fully mixed. The reaction was carried out for 20 min at 150 °C using a Microwave digestion at 1450 W. After taking out, it was cooled to room temperature collected and concentrated for further use. We chose quinine sulfate as a reference compound. The quantum yield of N,Zn-CDs is 14.26% calculated by following equation.
φ x
=
F x
*
A S
F x
*
A x
*
φ s
In above equation S and xrepresent the reference compound and measured compound. A is the absorbance at the excitation wavelength. F is the fluorescence integral area at the excitation wavelength.

N,Zn-CDs (8 mL) were dissolved in water (7 mL) and the solution was adjusted to pH 4, followed by addition of EDC (0.5 g) and NHS (0.5 g) for 1 h to activate the surface of N,Zn-CDs. β-CD (0.05 g) powder was added and stirred in a water bath at 80 °C for 24 h. Finally, prepared nanoprobe was filtered (0.22 μM) and dialysis bag (1000D) for 24 h used to remove unreacted β-CD, EDC and NHS. The β-CD-N,Zn-CDs nanoprobe was finally obtained for detection.

Since FQs have similar effects on the fluorescence of β-CD-N,Zn-CDs, so chose OFL as a representative for analysis. Briefly, an appropriate amount of OFL (0–10 μM) was dissolved in water, and a β-CD-N,Zn-CDs nanoprobe was added, followed by ultrasonication (10 min) of the test tube containing the mixture. Finally, the fluorescence spectrum was measured using a fluorescence spectrometer and the relevant data were recorded. In order to detect the specificity of the β-CD-N,Zn-CDs nanoprobe, other ions were added under the same conditions using the same experimental method, and antibiotics and small molecules were used to replace OFL. Emission intensity of λex = =350 nm was obtained for quantitative detection.

ce:section.ce:section-title: OFL detection in milk and domestic water sample
ce:section.ce:para: Domestic water comes from local sources, and not pre-treated following. Milk samples from local super-market, and pre-treated following. In short, milks (1 mL) and acetonitrile (4 mL) were added, and the mixtures were sonicated for 15 min. The samples were centrifuged (5000 rpm, 15 min) to remove the precipitate of denatured protein. The supernatants were collected and filtered through a 0.22 μM organic filter membrane to remove protein residues. The treated milk samples were diluted 10-fold (10 mM, pH7.4) with phosphate buffer, and containing different concentrations of OFL(0.08, 0.4 and 2 μM) were adding. Finally, the amount of OFL was calculated by β-CD-N,Zn-CDs fluorescence intensity and calibration curve.

ce:section.ce:section-title: TEM, XRD and XPS analysis of N,Zn-CDs
ce:section.ce:para: N,Zn-CDs nanoprobe characteristics were revealed by TEM, XPS, XRD and fluorescence spectra, respectively. The TEM images in Fig. 1 indicate that nanoprobes present monodisperse state, the size distribution of nanoprobes is concentrated at 4–5 nm. The formation of N,Zn-CDs was further confirmed by the position of the XRD peak coincident with the position of the graphene peak. The XPS images analysis in Fig. S1.

N,Zn-CDs forms an amide bond with β-CD. β-CD-N,Zn-CDs nanoprobes were characterised using TEM, FT-IR and fluorescence spectroscopy. Analyzing β-CD-N,Zn-CDs TEM image (Fig. S2) and FT-IR spectra in Fig. S3.

The β-CD-N,Zn-CDs produces an emission peak that depends on the excitation wavelength and a fluorescence spectrum obtained at different excitation wavelengths in the range of 300 nm to 420 nm (Fig. 2 a). Not only does the intensity of the fluorescence varies with the excitation wavelength, but the position of the peak also changes in the emission spectrum, indicating that the fluorescence emission produced by β-CD-N,Zn-CDs is actually related to excitation. Fig. 2b shows that as the excitation wavelength increases from 300 nm to 350 nm, the fluorescence intensity gradually increases. The 350–420 nm and the fluorescence intensity are gradually decreased. The excitation wavelength is the highest at 350 nm. Therefore we use 350 nm as the best excitation wavelength. Stability of β-CD-N,Zn-CDs is studied. The pH has influence on β-CD-N,Zn-CDs Fig. S4a. The experimental results show that the fluorescence intensity changes are small of β-CD-N,Zn-CDs under acidic conditions, and the fluorescence intensity was gradually weakened under alkaline conditions. The effect of ultrasonic time on β-CD-N,Zn-CDs showed that the fluorescence intensity increased with the increase of ultrasonic time Fig. S4b. When the time exceeded 10 min, the fluorescence intensity was not enhanced. The temperature has influence on β-CD-N,Zn-CDs Fig. S4c. The experimental results show that the fluorescence intensity increases with increasing temperature, and the fluorescence intensity does not increase when the temperature is increased to 60 °C.

Under optimized assay conditions, pH 7, temperature 25 °C and ultrasound (10 min). We selected OFL, NOR and CIP as analytical samples, respectively. OFL showed a linear relationship in the concentration range of 0.075–3.75 μM with a regression coefficient of 0.99 and a LOD of 0.05 μM. The F-F0/F0 regression equation of the calibration curve is y = =0.6175x − 0.0362 (Fig. S5). NOR and CIP are also given a linear relationship within a certain range (Figs. S6 and S7).

OFL enhanced the fluorescence intensity of N,Zn-CDs, but the fluorescence did not red shift (Fig. S8A). The surface of N,Zn-CDs is rich in carboxyl groups and hydroxyl groups. These advantages provide an opportunity for the formation of hydrogen bonds between OFL and N,Zn-CDs, which promote the production of larger fluorophores and produce more under the same excitation light, enhanced fluorescence [26,27]. The addition of Zn2+ ions is beneficial to increase the formation of hydrogen bonds between OFL and CDs, which are beneficial to the generation of fluorophores, due to fluorescence resonance energy transfer (FRET), energy is transferred from donors through non-radiative dipole-dipole interactions during FRET, as showing in Fig. S8B. Ex = =350, cyclodextrin fluorescence intensity is 119.414, OFL fluorescence intensity is 162.330, and the fluorescence intensity of the OFL and cyclodextrin mixture is 292.543, which shows that the OFL has no effect on the cyclodextrin fluorescence(Fig. S8C). β-CD forms an inclusion complex with N,Zn-CDs, the OFL concentration increases and the complex fluorescence does not red shift (Fig. S8D). The fluorescence intensity of β-CD-N,Zn-CDs increases with increasing OFL concentration, and the fluorescence spectrum is red shifted (Fig. S8E). This is because N,Zn-CDs are modified by amino-containing β-CD, and amide bonds are formed, β-CD not only increases the specific surface area of ​N,Zn-CDs, but also increases the degree of intramolecular charge transfer.

The fluorescence enhancement mechanism may be due to hydrogen bond interactions between the cyclodextrin cavity and the OFL and charge transfer between N,Zn-CDs and OFL. In one aspect, the fluorescent probe can be associated with a surface state. The surface of N,Zn-CDs is rich in carboxyl groups and hydroxyl groups. These advantages provide opportunities for water solubility and hydrogen bond-based interactions with cyclodextrin or OFL. There are data showing that β-CD bind to OFL to form a stable inclusion complex with a dissociation constant from 5 × 10−3 M to 10−5 M [28]. On the other hand, charge transfer also plays an important role. It can also be related to the electronic acceptability of the cyclodextrin as the host and the electron donating properties of the guest OFL. The conjugated system of side chain ethylenediamine groups can be transferred to N,Zn-CDs by charge. As showed in Fig. 3
a and b, the fluorescence and UV–Vis absorption of β-CD-N,Zn-CDs were significantly enhanced as compared with N,Zn-CDs. The synergy of hydrogen bonding and charge transfer promotes the generation of fluorophores and chromophores. The conjugate produces a stronger emission under the same excitation [26,27].

Milk and domestic water, which potentially possess various matrix effects. Such as salinity, water hardness, varied pH, and metal ions, these parameters could affect the fluorescence of the probe and thus negatively affect the fluorescence detection performance. For changes in these parameters, an assessment of the tolerance of the proposed method becomes essential to change these parameters.

Various parameters related to the sample must be considered, such as salinity, varying pH and toxic compound, as they may affect the nanoprobe and have a negative impact on fluorescence performance. Hence, the evaluation parameters become critical. Considering the case or actual sample, it was found that the pH value was in the range of pH 4.0–7.0, this analysis method was well tolerated, as showed in Fig. S4a. The effects of salinity were evaluated using KCl. CaCl2 and MgCl2 as model salt, and Fig. S9 show that the method is highly resistant to both K+ and Na+ ions. The influence of heavy metal, using FeCl3, CuCl2 and ZnSO4 as barriers, Fig. S9 shows that the method is resistant to Fe3+, Zn2+ and Cu2+ ions. Although the nano-probe fluorescence quenching after introduction of Fe3+ and Cu2+, It has less effect on the fluorescence detection of OFL.

ce:section.ce:section-title: Detection of antibiotics in actual samples

ce:section.ce:para: In summary, a novel functionalized carbon dots β-CD-N,Zn-CDs synthesized. The fluorescence detection of FQs is realized, and the fluorescent probe has excellent sensitivity and specificity. Satisfactory results were obtained after applying the probe to the actual sample. This study further confirmed that the presence of transition metal ions has the ability to enhance the binding of CDs to certain specific compounds. The presence of β-CD increases the degree of intramolecular charge transfer, which is beneficial to the red shift of fluorescence, and CDs serve as an excellent alternative to molecular fluorophore in fluorescence analysis.

We hereby confirm that this manuscript is our original work and has not been published nor has it been submitted simultaneously elsewhere. We further confirm that all authors have checked the manuscript and have agreed to the submission.

ce:conflict-of-interest.ce:para: The authors declare that they have no known competing financial interests or personal relationships that could have appeared to influence the work reported in this paper.

ce:section.ce:para: Supplementary material associated with this article can be found, in the online version, at doi:10.1016/j.microc.2019.104517.

sb:title.sb:maintitle: Selective sample pretreatment by molecularly imprinted polymer monolith for the analysis of fluoroquinolones from milk samples
J. Chromatogr. A

ce:bib-reference.ce:source-text: Zheng M.-M., Gong R., Zhao X., Feng Y.-Q.Selective sample pretreatment by molecularly imprinted polymer monolith for the analysis of fluoroquinolones from milk samples. J. Chromatogr. A. 2010;1217(14):2075–81.

sb:title.sb:maintitle: Novel optical PVC probes for on-site detection/determination of fluoroquinolones in a solid/liquid interface: application to the determination of Norfloxacin in aquaculture water
Biosens. Bioelectron.

ce:bib-reference.ce:source-text: Silva T.I., Moreira F.T., Truta L.A., Sales M.G.F.Novel optical PVC probes for on-site detection/determination of fluoroquinolones in a solid/liquid interface: application to the determination of Norfloxacin in aquaculture water. Biosens. Bioelectron.. 2012;36(1):199–206.
